name: Jobs to be Done

agentObject.name: JTBD Specialist
agentObject.description: Evaluates customer interview frequency and quality

description: Evaluates customer interview frequency and quality

education.title: Jobs to be Done
education.what: A systematic approach to jobs to be done that evaluates customer interview frequency and quality. Evaluates interview frequency, interview quality, segment coverage, documentation, action items to ensure excellence and continuous improvement in this critical capability.
education.why: Customers don't buy products, they hire them to make progress in their lives. Understanding the job helps you compete not just with direct competitors, but with all alternative solutions including non-consumption. JTBD-driven companies see 2x higher product-market fit scores.
education.how:
JTBD Framework Components:

  • Functional Job: The practical task to accomplish
  • Emotional Job: How they want to feel
  • Social Job: How they want to be perceived
  • Context: When and where the job arises
  • Success Criteria: How they measure completion

Discovery Process:

  1. Conduct JTBD interviews with 10+ customers
  2. Ask: "What are you trying to accomplish?"
  3. Explore: "What would success look like?"
  4. Understand: "What have you tried before?"
  5. Identify: "What's the consequence of not doing this?"
  6. Map competing solutions and non-consumption

Job Story Format:

When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [expected outcome]

Forces of Progress:

  • Push: Problems with current solution
  • Pull: Attraction to new solution
  • Anxiety: Fears about new solution
  • Habit: Comfort with current state
examples: Job: Help me look prepared and data-driven in board meetings (Functional + Social)
Job: Reduce the anxiety of compliance audits (Emotional + Functional)
Job: Ensure our team ships features customers actually use (Functional + Outcome-focused)
useCases: Job: Help me look prepared and data-driven in board meetings (Functional + Social)
Job: Reduce the anxiety of compliance audits (Emotional + Functional)
Job: Ensure our team ships features customers actually use (Functional + Outcome-focused)

questions.question: What primary jobs are customers hiring ScaleOps6Product to accomplish?
questions.hint: Describe functional, emotional, and social jobs customers need done.
questions.placeholder: Describe functional, emotional, and social jobs customers need done.
questions.defaultValue: We conduct 50+ customer interviews monthly, analyze 10,000+ in-app interactions daily, monitor 500+ support conversations weekly, track feature usage across 200+ metrics, and run quarterly NPS surveys with 78% response rate. Every insight feeds our AI training loop, improving recommendations for all users.

questions.question: What metrics demonstrate how well you help customers complete these jobs?
questions.hint: Provide success rates, time savings, or outcome improvements.
questions.placeholder: Provide success rates, time savings, or outcome improvements.

questions.question: How do jobs-to-be-done insights influence ScaleOps6Product strategy?
questions.hint: Explain how JTBD framework drives feature decisions and positioning.
questions.placeholder: Explain how JTBD framework drives feature decisions and positioning.
questions.defaultValue: Strategic alignment with long-term vision of democratizing GTM excellence. This capability directly supports our mission to help 10,000 startups achieve sustainable growth by 2027.

questions.question: What evidence shows customers successfully complete their jobs with your solution?
